que: What is the main purpose of Kotlin's `inline` modifier?
options: To reduce overhead in certain higher-order functions
To make a function private
To convert a function into a coroutine
To make any function recursive

que: Why can higher-order functions introduce runtime overhead?
options: They may create function objects and closures
They always block the main thread
They cannot return values
They disable smart casts
Copies the function body and inlinable lambdas into the call site
Moves the function into a different file
Creates more wrapper objects
Forces the function to run on `Dispatchers.IO`

que: Which kind of function benefits most from `inline`?
options: A small higher-order utility called often
A giant data class constructor
A JSON file in assets
An XML layout resource
To prevent a lambda parameter from being inlined
To prevent a function from compiling
To force recursion
To make a class immutable
Restricting non-local returns from a lambda
Running a lambda on multiple threads
Making a lambda nullable
Serializing a lambda

que: What is a tradeoff of using `inline`?
options: Generated code size can grow
The function stops returning values
It disables generics
It prevents testing

que: What is a poor use of `inline`?
options: Marking a large ordinary function inline without a clear reason
Using it for a small higher-order helper
Learning how it works
Measuring performance before optimizing
It is usually most relevant when lambdas are involved
It is required for all generic functions
It only works in Android apps
It replaces `suspend` functions

que: What should guide the decision to use `inline` for optimization?
options: Actual benefit and reasonable use, ideally backed by measurement
Adding it to every public function
Whether the file is short
Whether the function name is long
